title: podman cannot pull & skopeo cannot inspect images from nvcr.io (NVIDIA GPU Cloud)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:


# **kind bug**
**Description**

podman-0.9.4-dev, podman-0.9.2 cannot pull images from the NVIDIA GPU Cloud 
skopeo-0.1.31-dev cannot inspect images from the NVIDIA GPU Cloud

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pull  nvcr.io/nvidia/caffe2:18.08-py3`
2.  `skopeo inspect  docker://nvcr.io/nvidia/caffe2:18.08-py3`

**Describe the results you received:**

podman: 
```
Trying to pull nvcr.io/nvidia/caffe2:18.08-py3...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:8ee29e426c26c79e7ba03ccc8bbc7fe99db00ffcbccb679d9c643b5546d8dc8a
Failed
error pulling image "nvcr.io/nvidia/caffe2:18.08-py3": unable to pull nvcr.io/nvidia/caffe2:18.08-py3: unable to pull image: Error reading blob sha256:8ee29e426c26c79e7ba03ccc8bbc7fe99db00ffcbccb679d9c643b5546d8dc8a: Invalid status code returned when fetching blob 504
```
skopeo:
```
FATA[0063] Invalid status code returned when fetching blob 504 
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman-0.9.4-dev
podman-0.9.2
skopeo-0.1.31-dev
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.9.2-5.git37a2afe.el7_5.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.11.4, commit: cade65d40947e9d2c10294022cb672c4252b64e8-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "7.5"
  MemFree: 158547619840
  MemTotal: 257610862592
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-52.dev.git70ca035.el7_5.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 32
  hostname: ip-172-31-66-66.eu-central-1.compute.internal
  kernel: 3.10.0-935.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 341h 45m 49.03s (Approximately 14.21 days)
insecure registries:
  registries:
  - nvcr.io
registries:
  registries:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - nvcr.io
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

comments.body:
[I can’t reproduce this (apparently the registry requires authentication), anyway…]

HTTP status code 504 is “Gateway timeout”; that is usually a server-side issue the clients can’t do anything about.  At least comparing the successful `GET https://nvcr.io/v2/nvidia/caffe2/blobs/` with the failing one in the debug log, I can’t see any indication so far that the client is doing anything to cause the failure.

(c/image should definitely be improved include a `http.StatusText` for the HTTP status code, though.)
After several attempts / hours waiting it works with podman, skopeo.

> (c/image should definitely be improved include a `http.StatusText` for the HTTP status code, though.)

https://github.com/containers/image/pull/516
